title: narcissus cuatrecasasii
intro: Narcissus cuatrecasasii, also known as the Cuatrecasas' Daffodil, is a rare and endemic plant species native to Colombia, belonging to the Amaryllidaceae family. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly its alkaloids, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. The health benefits of Narcissus cuatrecasasii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cognitive function, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
